case_title: MELANIE HARRIS VS. JELANI, INC., A CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS IN et al

docket_entries.description: JURY FEES DEPOSITED BY PLAINTIFF HARRIS, MELANIE
OPPOSITION TO MTN FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT JELANI INC.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MELANIE HARRIS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED AS TO THESE PARTICULAR SUBPOENAS AND REQUEST FOR MEDICAL INFORMATION. THE SUBPOENAS DIRECTED TO HER FORMER EMPLOYERS ARE TOO BROADLY WORDED. BALANCING THE PRIVACY RIGHTS OF THE PLAINTIFF AGAINST DEFENDANTS' RIGHT TO PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE DEFENSE, THE SCOPE OF THE SUBPOENAS AND MEDICAL REQUESTS ARE WELL BEYOND THE DISCOVERY NECESSARY IN THIS CASE WHERE THE CLAIM IS SEXUAL HARASSMENT AS OPPOSED TO WRONGFUL TERMINATION BASED ON ALLEGED NON-PERFORMANCE. SIMILARLY, WHERE THE MEDICAL DAMAGE CLAIM ALLEGES MENTAL DISTRISS, THERE IS NO JUSTIFICATION FOR INQUIRY INTO HER ENTIRE MEDICAL HISTORY. IN BOTH INSTANCES, MORE NARROWLY DRAWN DISCOVERY REQUESTS WOULD BE APPROPRIATE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Set For Trial On Jul-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order;
Off Calendar Nov-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Winter Hankins, Esq. of the Law Offices of Daniel Feder, Tel. 415.391.9476, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF MELANIE HARRIS' Motion For A Protective Order is granted as to these particular subpoenas and request for medical information. The subpoenas directed to her former employers are too broadly worded. Balancing the privacy rights of the plaintiff against defendants' right to prepare an appropriate defense, the scope of the subpoenas and medical requests are well beyond the discovery necessary in this case where the claim is sexual harassment as opposed to wrongful termination based on alleged non-performance. Similarly, where the medical damage claim alleges mental distriss, there is no justification for inquiry into her entire medical history. In both instances, more narrowly drawn discovery requests would be appropriate. (Prevailing party to submit a form of order.) Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
